title: Morning digest artifact for the Telegram bot
description: Add a digest builder that aggregates research notes/Findings produced within a time window into a digest artifact (markdown + JSON) that the Telegram bot can later push as a silent scheduled message, plus `bober research digest --since <iso>`. Transport, polling, and message rendering are out of scope; this sprint produces only the content artifact.

successCriteria.description: buildDigest(since, now, deps) collects research runs whose generatedAt falls in [since, now] and returns a structure listing each run's job title and its top finding.
`bober research digest --since <iso>` writes both .bober/research/digests/<date>.md and <date>.json, where the markdown lists each in-window run by title and the JSON is machine-readable for the bot.
An empty window produces a digest whose body states that no new research was produced in the window, written to both files rather than throwing or leaving an empty file.
The project compiles with the digest module and the `digest` subcommand registered on the research command.

nonGoals: Do not implement the Telegram bot, long-polling, user whitelist, inline buttons, or message sending (owned by the telegram sibling spec).
Do not embed sensitive/raw domain detail in the digest — non-sensitive summary content only (Telegram is not E2E-encrypted).
Do not change the runner, cadence, or egress behavior of earlier sprints.
Do not schedule the digest itself; it is produced on demand via the CLI (tick integration is optional and additive).
stopConditions: npm run build passes and tests assert: in-window runs aggregated with titles + top finding, both .md and .json digest files written, and an empty-window digest stating no new research.
`bober research digest --since <iso>` produces the two artifact files under .bober/research/digests/.
definitionOfDone: Running `bober research digest --since <iso>` collects every research run produced between since and now, writes a human-readable markdown digest and a machine-readable JSON digest under .bober/research/digests/<date>.{md,json}, listing each run's job title and top finding, and emits an explicit no-new-research digest when the window is empty. The digest holds only non-sensitive summary content. Build is green and earlier sprint tests still pass.
assumptions: The digest is the content artifact only; the Telegram bot (sibling spec) consumes the JSON and pushes it as a silent scheduled message (resolved per scope split). Evidence: research doc L140 (silent scheduled messages), L137-L141 (telegram is a presentation adapter sequenced after the hub).
Digest source data is the set of research notes/Findings produced by the Sprint 2 runner; the builder takes an injected collector so it is testable without real runs.
Non-sensitive summary only — no raw PHI/financial detail in the digest. Evidence: research doc L141 (telegram non-E2E privacy boundary).
generatorNotes: Create src/research/digest.ts with buildDigest(since, now, deps) where deps = {collectRuns(since, now)} returning the in-window runs (each carrying job title, generatedAt, and top finding). Render two outputs: a markdown body (heading + bullet per run: title, top finding, source link) and a JSON object {since, now, generatedAt, runs:[...]} for the bot. Write them to .bober/research/digests/<date>.md and <date>.json (ensureDir from src/state/helpers.ts). When deps.collectRuns returns an empty array, render a body that explicitly states no new research was produced in the window and still write both files. Add a `digest` subcommand with `--since <iso>` (default: last 24h computed at the CLI boundary) to src/cli/commands/research.ts. Keep the digest content non-sensitive (titles + summaries, not raw values) per research doc L141.
evaluatorNotes: Inject collectRuns returning two fake runs and assert the markdown lists both titles and each top finding, and the JSON parses with a 2-element runs array (sc-5-1, sc-5-2). Assert both .md and .json files are written under a temp .bober/research/digests/ (sc-5-2). Inject collectRuns returning [] and assert the written body contains the no-new-research statement and both files exist (sc-5-3). Confirm the `digest` subcommand is registered and npm run build passes.
